title: detect_usage_limit false-positives on a blocked stage whose output mentions usage limits (self-halts the backlog)
description:
## Problem

The circuit breaker's quota detection (`adw/safety.py` `_evaluate`) opens the
circuit with `USAGE_LIMIT_HALT_REASON` whenever a stage did **not** succeed AND
`detect_usage_limit(raw_output + stderr)` matches. The non-success gate
(`stage_succeeded = result.status is not None and result.status.outcome == "success"`)
was added (S-015/S-018) so a stage's own prose about usage limits could not
self-trip the matcher. But a legitimate **`blocked`** outcome is also
non-success, so a stage that *correctly* reports `blocked` and whose output
happens to contain a usage-limit phrase is falsely classified as a provider
quota halt — and a bogus fallback cooldown (`now + usage_limit_cooldown_minutes`)
is stamped, pausing the whole backlog.

## Evidence (GH-56, 2026-06-22 12:26Z overnight run)

- GH-56's plan stage **succeeded** (exit 0, no timeout, full 12.7 KB plan, 26,385
  tokens) and returned `outcome: blocked` for a real reason (a `feat` ticket
  cannot edit harness dirs; see companion note below).
- Its plan output contains "...run for hours (until the ~5h **session limit**)",
  which matches the `\bsession limit\b` pattern in `_USAGE_LIMIT_PATTERNS`.
- `not stage_succeeded` is True (outcome is `blocked`), so detection fired ->
  ticket marked `quotad`, cooldown stamped as the **fallback** `now + 300min`
  (`2026-06-22T17:31:55.856290Z` = run-end + exactly 5h; no real reset was
  parsed — confirming no genuine provider message was ever received).
- That bogus 5-hour cooldown then blocked every subsequent poll (12:34Z, 13:34Z:
  "circuit cooldown active"), so GH-58 and GH-59 never ran.

This is a recurrence of the 2026-06-19 false positive in a new shape (a `blocked`
outcome, not a `success` one).

## Approach (proposed)

A genuine provider usage limit cuts the CLI turn off mid-stream, so the stage
cannot return ANY parseable structured status block — `result.status is None`.
Gate detection on that, not on "non-success":

- Change the guard from `not stage_succeeded` to `result.status is None` (no
  verdict at all = genuinely cut off), and/or scan only `result.stderr` / the
  `rate_limit_event` CLI signal, never the agent's `raw_output` prose.

Prefer the `result.status is None` gate — minimal and it matches the real
cut-off semantics.
acceptance_criteria: A stage that returns ANY structured outcome (`blocked`, `done`, `success`,
A genuine cut-off (`result.status is None`) carrying a usage-limit signal still
Unit test: a `blocked` StageResult whose `raw_output` contains "session limit"
Full suite stays green (`uv run pytest -q`).
**Filed human-gated (no `adw` label) on purpose:** this fix sits in the very
**Companion finding (separate, not this ticket):** GH-56 is mis-typed `feat`
